---
title: "Intro to CoreHPC"
subtitle: "Part 2"
author: "Natalie Gill"
institute: "Gladstone Bioinformatics Core"
date: "TBD"
date-format: MMMM DD, YYYY
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: corehpc-theme.scss
    highlight: default
    code-line-numbers: false
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
---

```{r, setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(comment = "")
```

##

<center>*Press the ? key for tips on navigating these slides*</center>

## Introductions


&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;**Natalie Gill**      
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;*Bioinformatician II*      


## Target Audience

-   Prior experience with UNIX command-line
-   No prior experience on computing clusters


## Part 2:

1.    SLURM Basics
2.    Submitting Batch Jobs
3.    Requesting CPU, Memory, GPU Resources
4.    Job Arrays
5.    Interactive Jobs
6.    Monitoring & Canceling Jobs
7.    Running Containers in SLURM Jobs
8.    MPI / Multi-Node Jobs
9.    Pipelines on CoreHPC
10.   Jupyter / OpenOnDemand
11.   Best Practices and Troubleshooting
12.   How to Get Help


# SLURM Basics

## What is SLURM? {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: workload manager, replaces SGE on Wynton, supports interactive jobs and GPUs natively -->

## Key Concepts {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: login node vs compute nodes vs partitions vs job vs job script, srun inside sbatch -->

## Common Commands

<!-- TODO: table of sbatch, salloc, srun, squeue, scancel, sinfo, scontrol show job, sacct -->


# Submitting Batch Jobs

## Minimal Job Script

<!-- TODO: hello.sh example (#!/bin/bash, #SBATCH --job-name, #SBATCH --output, srun echo "Hello") -->

## Submitting

<!-- TODO: sbatch hello.sh, squeue -u $USER, output file -->


# Resource Requests

## CPU and Memory {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: --ntasks, --cpus-per-task, --mem, --time, --partition example -->

## GPU Jobs {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: --partition=gpu (or pod), --gres=gpu:1, module load cuda, what GPU types are available -->

## Pod Partition Note

<!-- TODO: Pod is request-only, 48 hr max wall-time, 5760 GPU hr allocation, multi-GPU shared memory -->


# Job Arrays

## When to Use

<!-- TODO: many similar tasks indexed by ID (per-sample, per-chromosome) -->

## Example Array Job

<!-- TODO: #SBATCH --array=1-100, %A / %a output format, $SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID -->


# Interactive Jobs

## salloc {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: salloc --partition=cpu --cpus-per-task=4 --mem=8G --time=01:00:00 -->

## Attaching to a Running Job

<!-- TODO: srun --jobid=<JOBID> --nodelist=<node> --pty /bin/bash to inspect a running job (e.g. nvidia-smi) -->


# Monitoring & Canceling

## Checking Status

<!-- TODO: squeue, squeue -u $USER, scontrol show job <jobid>, sacct -j <jobid> -->

## Job / Node Status Codes {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: table of idle, mix, down, drain, comp, etc. -->

## Canceling

<!-- TODO: scancel <jobid>, scancel -u $USER -->


# Containers in SLURM Jobs

## Running Apptainer in a Job

<!-- TODO: module load apptainer, srun apptainer exec <image>.sif <command> -->

## GPU-Enabled Containers

<!-- TODO: srun apptainer exec --nv <image>.sif python train.py, requires --gres=gpu:N -->

## Hybrid Host + Container Workflow

<!-- TODO: preprocess outside container, run analysis inside container, all in one job script -->


# MPI / Multi-Node Jobs

## MPI Job Script {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: --nodes=N, --ntasks-per-node=M, module load openmpi5/5.0.x or nvidia/nvhpc, srun mpi_program -->

## Important: Community CPU Partition

<!-- TODO: Community CPU partition does NOT have InfiniBand and is not for tightly-coupled MPI; use CPU/GPU partitions for that -->


# Pipelines on CoreHPC

## Nextflow with SLURM

<!-- TODO: nextflow runs on login node, executor 'slurm' profile, note that pipelines must be adapted from Wynton SGE configs -->

## No Internet on Compute Nodes

<!-- TODO: stage all containers / reference data / pipeline assets from the login node first, compute nodes are offline -->


# Jupyter / OpenOnDemand

## OpenOnDemand Portal

<!-- TODO: web portal entry point for Jupyter, RStudio, shell, etc. (replaces SSH port-forwarding dance from Wynton) -->

## Conda Kernels in Jupyter {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: conda install ipykernel, python -m ipykernel install --user --name myenv --display-name "Python (My Env)", jupyter kernelspec list -->


# Best Practices and Troubleshooting

## Best Practices {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: test interactively first, request only what you need, set --time, no compute on login/bastion, no internet on compute (stage first), prefer FAC over scratch for persistence -->

## Common Errors {.small-bullets}

<!-- TODO: pending due to resource limits, OOM, module not found, container missing --nv -->


## Poll 3

<!-- TODO: poll question about partitions or login-node use -->


# How to Get Help

## CoreHPC Support

- Support request form: <https://tiny.ucsf.edu/Gethpc>
- CoreHPC Slack channel (request access via the form above)

## Bioinformatics Questions

For any bioinformatics specific questions feel free to reach out to the Gladstone Bioinformatics Core.

-   Email
    -   [bioinformatics@gladstone.ucsf.edu](mailto:bioinformatics@gladstone.ucsf.edu)
-   Slack channel #questions-about-bioinformatics
    -   Contact us at the email above to be added to the channel


# End of Part 2

## Notes on Using AI on CoreHPC {.smaller}

<!-- TODO: AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) can help write/debug SLURM scripts. CoreHPC scratch is volatile (30 day) - understand any AI-generated script before running it. Bioinformatics Core AI position paper link. -->

## Workshop survey

- Please fill out our [workshop survey](TBD) so we can continue to improve these workshops

## Upcoming Data Science Training Program Workshops {.smaller}

<!-- TODO: fill in upcoming workshop list near the workshop date -->
